headline: CONFIRMATION with documented EMP signal. April 23 Microsoft announced first-ever voluntary retirement buyout in 51-year company history: ~7% US workforce / ~8,750 employees, eligibility 'Rule of 70' (age + tenure ≥ 70), senior director and below, sales-incentive plans excluded. Buyout structure is materially less coercive than Meta/Oracle direct layoffs — voluntary participation, retains health/severance benefits, targets long-tenured employees who can self-select. However TheNextWeb characterizes program as 'a buyout dressed as a benefit'; Rule of 70 disproportionately targets institutional knowledge holders in their 50s-60s. Net EMP effect: small negative calibration available (-0.2 to -0.3) but does not cross 5-point composite threshold. Live composite 66.4 (Established band, NOT 76.8 as user input claimed) holds.

evidence.finding: First-ever voluntary buyout program; up to 7% of US workforce / ~8,750 employees; Rule of 70 eligibility; senior director and below; sales-incentive plans excluded.
Rule of 70 disproportionately targets long-tenured employees in 50s-60s — institutional knowledge holders.
Characterized as 'a buyout dressed as a benefit'; AI reshape rationale.
Voluntary mechanism contrasts with Meta layoffs and Oracle 30K cold-emails (March 31). Less coercive structurally.

data_integrity_flag: User input claimed Microsoft live = 76.8 (held). Live site/src/data/indexes/fortune-500.json shows Microsoft composite = 66.4. Used live deterministic baseline per assessor instruction. Recommend rotation-state reconciliation.
methodologyNote: Voluntary buyout structure is qualitatively distinct from Meta/Oracle direct layoffs — assessor scoring framework should consider voluntariness as EMP modifier in future methodology iterations. Microsoft remains at established band (66.4) with strong AWR/ACT/SYS profile and modest EMP calibration available but not warranting proposal.
